rationale: Both correctly diagnose the removed get_cache_scale call and correctly identify the fix (drop the branch, rely on maybe_remap_kv_scale_name), matching the ground truth's disposition of closing as a duplicate pointing to #4808. X more precisely mirrors the actual resolution ('duplicate of #4808'), catches a real inconsistency (that #4808 shows closed/unmerged) and hedges appropriately rather than asserting it's already fixed on main, which is exactly the kind of epistemic care that should be rewarded on an issue this terse. Y instead flatly claims 'the current main tip' already has the fix and tells the user to git pull, while elsewhere calling #4808 'closed', an unhedged and internally inconsistent claim that could mislead the reporter. X's added flag about the regression-test coverage gap and the merge-status risk also makes it more useful end-to-end for the reporter's actual situation.
